# Issue Backlog

Pre-cut issues ready to file. Copy any block into a new GitHub issue.
Organized by category. Each entry has: title, why, acceptance criteria,
technical notes, labels, difficulty.

Legend — difficulty:
- 🟢 easy — 1–3 h, no new abstractions
- 🟡 medium — half a day to a day, touches 2–4 files
- 🔴 hard — multi-day, design decisions involved

---

## Documentation

### D1. Record and embed demo GIF
**Why:** README has a placeholder. A 15s loop showing CLI → "remember …" → web UI is the highest-leverage README change for adoption.
**Acceptance:** `docs/demo.gif` exists, under 5 MB, referenced from README. Shows: cold start, one chat, one memory save, opening web UI.
**Notes:** Tools like `terminalizer`, `asciinema-agg`, or screen recording + `ffmpeg -i in.mp4 -vf "fps=15,scale=900:-1" out.gif`.
**Labels:** documentation, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### D2. Add Mermaid architecture diagram to ARCHITECTURE.md
**Why:** The ASCII diagram is fine but Mermaid renders on GitHub and is easier to extend.
**Acceptance:** New top-level Mermaid `graph TD` showing UI → engine → providers/memory/tools/plugins/MCP, plus a sequence diagram of the agent loop.
**Notes:** Keep the ASCII version below as a fallback.
**Labels:** documentation, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### D3. "Build your first plugin" tutorial
**Why:** PLUGINS.md is reference, not narrative. A tutorial converts curious users into plugin authors.
**Acceptance:** `docs/tutorials/first-plugin.md`, walks from `mkdir plugins/hello` to seeing the tool in `/tools`, in under 5 minutes.
**Notes:** Reuse the `hello-world` example.
**Labels:** documentation, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### D4. "Connect your first MCP server" tutorial
**Why:** MCP.md is reference. A walkthrough for `mcp-server-fetch` or `mcp-server-filesystem` shortens the on-ramp.
**Acceptance:** `docs/tutorials/first-mcp.md`, copy-paste config that works against one of the official MCP example servers.
**Labels:** documentation, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### D5. Per-OS install troubleshooting page
**Why:** Windows ARM64, macOS gatekeeper, Linux SELinux all hit different sharp edges. One page collects them.
**Acceptance:** `docs/INSTALL.md`, sections per OS, each with the 2–3 most common errors and the fix.
**Labels:** documentation, help wanted
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### D6. CONTRIBUTORS.md generated from git log
**Why:** Recognition matters; manual list rots.
**Acceptance:** `CONTRIBUTORS.md` generated by a script in `scripts/`, regenerated by maintainers on each release.
**Notes:** Use `git shortlog -sne` then dedupe.
**Labels:** documentation, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### D7. Add a Mermaid sequence diagram of memory retrieval
**Why:** The hybrid retrieval scoring is the most opaque part of the system; a diagram helps users debug "why didn't it remember X".
**Acceptance:** Diagram in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` showing query → embed → vector search → rank (70/20/10) → injection.
**Labels:** documentation
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### D8. Provider walkthrough screencasts (linked from PROVIDERS.md)
**Why:** A 30-second video per provider beats a paragraph of setup text.
**Acceptance:** Links to 30s screencasts for Ollama, Anthropic, OpenAI, Groq, Gemini.
**Labels:** documentation
**Difficulty:** 🟡

---

## Frontend (Web UI)

### F1. Mobile-tuned chat view
**Why:** The shell is responsive (`#panel { display: none }` ≤ 900 px), but composer keyboard handling, scroll lock, and tap targets are desktop-tuned.
**Acceptance:** Chat is usable on a phone — virtual keyboard doesn't hide the composer, tool pills wrap, command palette works as full-screen modal on small viewports.
**Labels:** frontend, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### F2. Prompt history (↑/↓ in composer)
**Why:** Every CLI has it; the web composer doesn't. One ergonomic step from "good" to "obvious".
**Acceptance:** ↑/↓ in the empty composer cycles through the last 50 user messages of the current session.
**Notes:** State already arrays in `state.lastUserMsg`; just hold the full list.
**Labels:** frontend, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### F3. Light theme toggle
**Why:** Dark is great; light is essential for some users and demos.
**Acceptance:** Toggle in Settings, persists to `localStorage`, no flash-of-wrong-theme on reload.
**Notes:** Tokens already at the top of the stylesheet — flip them in a `:root[data-theme=light]` rule.
**Labels:** frontend, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### F4. Markdown table rendering
**Why:** Model output often uses `|` tables; renderer currently dumps them as paragraphs.
**Acceptance:** Standard GFM tables render with borders, dim header row, monospace cell content.
**Labels:** frontend, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### F5. KaTeX math rendering
**Why:** Tutoring and STEM use cases need math.
**Acceptance:** `$x^2 + y^2 = z^2$` and `$$\sum_{i=0}^{n} i^2$$` render via KaTeX; falls back to raw on parse failure.
**Notes:** Vendor KaTeX CSS+JS as static asset; do not pull from a CDN (offline-first).
**Labels:** frontend, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### F6. Image preview for MCP tool results
**Why:** MCP servers can return image blocks; the renderer ignores them.
**Acceptance:** Tool results with `type: 'image'` blocks render as `<img>` inside the tool result panel.
**Notes:** Validate `data:` URLs; reject non-image MIME types.
**Labels:** frontend, security
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### F7. Session search in panel
**Why:** Once a user has 30+ sessions, scrolling fails.
**Acceptance:** Input above `#sess-list`; filters by name + preview substring.
**Labels:** frontend, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### F8. Keyboard-shortcuts modal
**Why:** Discoverability of Ctrl+K, ↑↓ in composer, send/newline.
**Acceptance:** `?` opens a modal listing every shortcut. Lives in `state.commands` so future shortcuts auto-appear.
**Labels:** frontend, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### F9. Inline citation links for `web_search` results
**Why:** Right now the model summarizes; the user can't click through.
**Acceptance:** When a `web_search` tool result is in context, the assistant's response renders `[1]` superscripts that link to the source URLs.
**Notes:** Engine already passes structured results; client just needs to keep a per-message URL list.
**Labels:** frontend, ai
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### F10. Streaming code blocks with progressive syntax highlight
**Why:** Code blocks currently render plain until the closing ```. Streaming highlight feels alive.
**Acceptance:** Highlight applies as soon as the language tag is seen and updates incrementally.
**Notes:** highlight.js or Shiki; size-budget the bundle.
**Labels:** frontend, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### F11. Conversation branching (regenerate from any message)
**Why:** Editing a user message and resending is currently destructive.
**Acceptance:** "Edit" on a user message rewinds context to that point and lets the user re-send.
**Notes:** Needs a `truncate(messageIndex)` method on `ConversationContext`.
**Labels:** frontend, ai, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### F12. Drag-to-pin conversations
**Why:** Important threads get lost in the sorted-by-time list.
**Acceptance:** Pin icon on session items; pinned sessions stick to the top.
**Labels:** frontend, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### F13. Voice input placeholder button
**Why:** Sets up the v1.x voice milestone visually without shipping STT.
**Acceptance:** Mic button in the composer; clicking shows "Voice arrives in v1.x" toast and the GitHub roadmap link.
**Labels:** frontend, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### F14. "Open in editor" buttons for code blocks
**Why:** Copy is good; opening in `code:` URL handler or downloading as a file is better.
**Acceptance:** Each code block has Copy + Download. Download infers extension from the language tag.
**Labels:** frontend, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### F15. Render `<details>` tool results collapsed by default
**Why:** Long tool outputs steal the chat view.
**Acceptance:** Tool result panel starts collapsed; clicking expands. Long-result indicator shows char count.
**Labels:** frontend, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🟢

---

## Backend / Architecture

### B1. Dockerfile + docker-compose for PersonalAI + Ollama
**Why:** "Try it in 30 seconds" with a single `docker compose up`.
**Acceptance:** `Dockerfile` (multi-stage), `docker-compose.yml` with Ollama service, README install section gains a Docker tab.
**Notes:** Persist `~/.personal-ai` as a named volume; bind 127.0.0.1 only.
**Labels:** backend, devops, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### B2. VS Code devcontainer
**Why:** One-click contributor setup with all toolchains pinned.
**Acceptance:** `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` + `Dockerfile` install Node 20, pull baseline models if `OLLAMA_PREFETCH` is set, run `npm install` on attach.
**Labels:** backend, devops, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### B3. Configurable conversation budget via env
**Why:** `MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS` is hardcoded at 24 K. Users with 128 K-context models want to raise it; 8 GB users want to lower it.
**Acceptance:** `MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS` env override read at startup; `/cost` or `/health` shows the active value.
**Labels:** backend, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### B4. Per-tool rate limiting
**Why:** Defense in depth on top of the confirmation gate. A buggy or hostile model could trigger N file_reader confirms in a row.
**Acceptance:** New `rateLimit: { perMinute: number }` field on `RegisteredTool`; registry tracks call counts; over-limit calls return a "rate limited" `ToolResult`.
**Labels:** backend, security
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### B5. Opt-in anonymous telemetry
**Why:** Maintainers need to know which providers, which features are used. Never message content.
**Acceptance:** `TELEMETRY=on` opts in. Sends provider name, model family, feature flags. Receiver: a future endpoint (start with logging to disk).
**Notes:** Must be opt-in, never on by default. Document fully in SECURITY.md.
**Labels:** backend, privacy, architecture
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### B6. Provider streaming for Anthropic tool blocks
**Why:** Currently the assistant waits for the full assistant message before yielding `tool_call` chunks on Anthropic.
**Acceptance:** Stream Anthropic `content_block_start` / `_delta` / `_stop` events into the unified `ChatChunk` shape; native tool calls appear progressively.
**Labels:** backend, ai
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### B7. Anthropic native tool_use/tool_result threading
**Why:** Better quality on Claude when the model can pair its own tool_use with the result.
**Acceptance:** When the prior assistant message contains tool_use blocks, thread the matching tool_result blocks back as native `role: 'user'` content blocks (not orphan text).
**Notes:** Compatibility: fall back to text downgrade when the conversation has no prior tool_use (e.g., loaded sessions).
**Labels:** backend, ai
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### B8. Auto-summarized memory rollups
**Why:** Memory grows monotonically. Old low-importance memories should compact.
**Acceptance:** Background job (manual `/memory rollup` first; later cron-style): groups same-type, low-importance, low-access memories; produces a single summary memory; archives originals.
**Labels:** ai, backend, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### B9. Plugin hot-reload on file change
**Why:** Developers currently `/plugins reload` manually.
**Acceptance:** `chokidar` watch on plugin entry files; debounce 500 ms; auto-reload that plugin.
**Notes:** Opt-in via env so production users aren't surprised.
**Labels:** backend, plugins, developer-experience
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### B10. Plugin isolation via worker_threads
**Why:** True isolation — currently a plugin can read `.env` despite the file-reader allowlist.
**Acceptance:** Plugins spawned in workers; tool/hook calls cross via `MessagePort`.
**Notes:** API change for plugin authors (no shared filesystem helpers); ship behind a flag first.
**Labels:** security, architecture, plugins
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### B11. Web server graceful shutdown
**Why:** Ctrl+C currently kills mid-stream; the WS client sees a hard close.
**Acceptance:** SIGINT closes the listener, sends `{type:'shutdown'}` to all WS, drains for ≤5 s, then exits.
**Labels:** backend, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### B12. WS reconnect with state restoration
**Why:** On reconnect, the client loses any in-flight stream.
**Acceptance:** WS reconnect resumes the same conversation context on the server side; UI restores the last visible assistant message intact.
**Notes:** Server-side context is per-connection today; this requires a connection-id token.
**Labels:** backend, frontend
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### B13. CSP headers on web UI
**Why:** Defense in depth on top of the loopback-only bind.
**Acceptance:** `Content-Security-Policy` allowing `'self'` only; `default-src` strict; no inline event handlers.
**Notes:** Inline `<script>` becomes nonce-based or moves to a static file.
**Labels:** security, frontend
**Difficulty:** 🟡

---

## AI / Memory

### A1. Knowledge graph layer
**Why:** Flat memories don't capture relationships ("Nandan → studies at → IIT Dhanbad"). A graph layer enables timeline + topic clustering.
**Acceptance:** `src/memory/graph.ts` with `Entity`, `Relation` tables; `/memory graph <query>` renders a small subgraph in CLI.
**Notes:** Start tiny — entities only when a memory explicitly mentions them.
**Labels:** ai, architecture
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### A2. Topic clustering on memories
**Why:** A user with 500 memories wants "what do I know about X?" grouped, not listed.
**Acceptance:** k-means on memory embeddings; cluster labels generated from medoid memory content; visible in web UI memory view as collapsible groups.
**Labels:** ai, frontend
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### A3. User profile synthesis
**Why:** A composite "here's who the user is" summary is more useful than scrolling memories.
**Acceptance:** `/profile synth` produces a 200-word third-person summary of the user across all stored memories.
**Notes:** Cache; regenerate when ≥10 new memories arrive.
**Labels:** ai, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### A4. Memory timeline view
**Why:** "When did I last work on X?" needs a temporal axis, not just sort.
**Acceptance:** Web UI: Memory tab gains a timeline strip across the top; clicking a slice scopes the grid to that period.
**Labels:** frontend, ai
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### A5. Goal tracking primitive
**Why:** "Remind me about X every Monday" needs first-class goal entities.
**Acceptance:** New memory type `goal` with fields `targetDate`, `status`. CLI `/goals` lists active.
**Labels:** ai, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### A6. Multi-agent profile orchestration
**Why:** Profiles exist; orchestrating them ("coder writes; researcher critiques") is the next step.
**Acceptance:** New "swarm" profile that internally invokes sub-profiles and merges output.
**Notes:** Token budget is the main risk — cap iterations.
**Labels:** ai, architecture
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### A7. Vision input passthrough
**Why:** Most providers already support image input; PersonalAI just doesn't expose it.
**Acceptance:** Composer accepts dragged images; engine passes them to the provider when supported, with a visible "[image]" placeholder in chat history.
**Labels:** frontend, ai
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### A8. Local document RAG
**Why:** "Chat with my files" — the embeddings infra is already there.
**Acceptance:** New tool `read_documents` that indexes a directory; queries hit the same vector store with a separate collection.
**Notes:** Embedding cost is the main concern; chunk on paragraph boundaries.
**Labels:** ai, backend
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### A9. Citation rendering in chat
**Why:** Web search and (future) RAG results deserve [1] [2] inline links.
**Acceptance:** Engine emits citation markers in stream; client renders superscript links into a sources list at the message footer.
**Labels:** frontend, ai
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### A10. Better task-detection heuristics
**Why:** Length + keyword regex is crude. "Translate this paragraph" routes to `quick`.
**Acceptance:** Replace `detectTask` regex with a small classifier (token n-grams against task-labeled examples). Falls back to current regex when unsure.
**Notes:** Keep deterministic — no extra LLM call per message.
**Labels:** ai, enhancement
**Difficulty:** 🟡

---

## Tools & Plugins

### T1. Example plugin: weather
**Why:** Showcases a network-using tool that's safe (no auth, read-only).
**Acceptance:** `plugins/weather/` calls `open-meteo.com`; registers `current_weather(city)`.
**Labels:** plugins, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### T2. Example plugin: GitHub read-only
**Why:** Shows a plugin with one env var; the most-requested integration.
**Acceptance:** `plugins/github/` reads `GITHUB_TOKEN`; registers `github_search_issues`, `github_repo_info`.
**Labels:** plugins, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### T3. Example plugin: calendar (icalendar URL → upcoming events)
**Why:** Personal AI without calendar is incomplete.
**Acceptance:** `plugins/calendar/` reads `CALENDAR_URL`; registers `upcoming_events(n)`.
**Labels:** plugins, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### T4. Plugin marketplace pointer page
**Why:** Plugins are pointless without discovery.
**Acceptance:** `docs/AWESOME-PLUGINS.md` lists community plugins; PR template gives clear contribution rules.
**Labels:** documentation, plugins, community
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### T5. Plugin signing
**Why:** Even with the "plugins are trusted" model, signed manifests help when sharing.
**Acceptance:** Optional `signature` field in `plugin.json`; CLI warns when loading an unsigned third-party plugin.
**Notes:** Use `node:crypto` sign/verify with maintainer-supplied public keys.
**Labels:** security, plugins
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### T6. MCP server health check loop
**Why:** Spawned servers can die quietly; tools then 30s-timeout.
**Acceptance:** Background ping every 30 s; mark unreachable servers `failed` and re-attempt connect every 5 min.
**Labels:** mcp, reliability
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### T7. MCP server auto-discovery from `claude_desktop_config.json`
**Why:** Many users already have an MCP config from Claude Desktop. Reading it lowers their setup time to zero.
**Acceptance:** If `MCP_IMPORT_CLAUDE_DESKTOP=on`, read the standard Claude Desktop location and merge servers into the in-memory config.
**Labels:** mcp, integrations
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### T8. Built-in `web_fetch` tool
**Why:** Models often want to read a URL, not search. Less search-API friction.
**Acceptance:** New `web_fetch(url)` tool with `requiresConfirmation: true`; respects a `WEB_FETCH_DOMAINS` allowlist.
**Labels:** tools, security
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### T9. Better tool result presentation
**Why:** Right now tool results are JSON dumps. Pretty-print for known shapes.
**Acceptance:** Tool result renderer pretty-prints search results (title + URL + snippet), notes (markdown), tasks (table).
**Labels:** frontend, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

---

## Security

### S1. Optional disk encryption for memory.db
**Why:** Memories can include sensitive context. WAL files inherit the journal mode but not user-level encryption.
**Acceptance:** `MEMORY_PASSPHRASE` env (read once at boot); when set, use `better-sqlite3-multiple-ciphers` or `node-sqlcipher`.
**Notes:** Migration path: re-encrypt on first set; fail loud if passphrase wrong.
**Labels:** security, architecture
**Difficulty:** 🔴

### S2. Audit log of every tool call
**Why:** Users should be able to see "what did the assistant do with my filesystem this week?"
**Acceptance:** `~/.personal-ai/audit.log` append-only; one line per tool call: timestamp, name, hash of args, success.
**Labels:** security
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### S3. Limit per-message memory writes
**Why:** A misbehaving model could spam memory.
**Acceptance:** Max N (default 5) memory writes per assistant turn; further ones logged and dropped.
**Labels:** security, ai
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### S4. CSP + Subresource Integrity for web UI
**Why:** No external assets today, but `<script>` is inline. Lock it down.
**Acceptance:** CSP header; `<script>` nonce-based or moved to `/app.js`.
**Labels:** security, frontend
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### S5. Configurable timeout per tool
**Why:** A blocked MCP server can stall the chat for 30 s.
**Acceptance:** Per-tool `timeoutMs` field on `RegisteredTool`; default 30 s; surfaces as `tool_result` with `error: 'timeout'`.
**Labels:** security, reliability
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### S6. Strict CORS allowlist
**Why:** Currently `cors({ origin: localhost:PORT })`, but the Host check is the real defense. Make CORS strict too.
**Acceptance:** Reject any cross-origin request; document remote-proxy setup.
**Labels:** security, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

---

## Testing

### TST1. Playwright e2e smoke for web UI
**Why:** The web UI has no automated end-to-end coverage.
**Acceptance:** One test: boot server, open page with token, send "hi", assert assistant message appears.
**Notes:** Mock provider via env or a test-only WebServerOptions.
**Labels:** testing, frontend
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### TST2. Provider integration smoke tests via mock HTTP servers
**Why:** Each provider has a mock-fetch unit test; an integration test against a local HTTP mock catches actual wire-format bugs.
**Acceptance:** New `tests/providers/integration/*.test.ts`, each boots a tiny `http.createServer`, drives `chat()` end to end.
**Labels:** testing
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### TST3. Memory fuzz
**Why:** Tokenized search regexes; near-duplicate dedupe; semantic edge cases.
**Acceptance:** Property-based tests (fast-check) for `search()`, `save()` idempotence under whitespace/case noise.
**Labels:** testing
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### TST4. Plugin sandbox stress
**Why:** Many plugins running many hooks should not OOM or deadlock.
**Acceptance:** Test with 50 plugins each defining all 7 hooks; ensure boot < 1 s and per-message hook overhead < 50 ms.
**Labels:** testing, plugins
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### TST5. Snapshot tests for markdown renderer
**Why:** The XSS-safe markdown function is critical; regressions here are security incidents.
**Acceptance:** Snapshot tests covering: code blocks, tables (after F4), nested lists, raw `<script>` (must escape).
**Labels:** testing, security, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### TST6. CLI commands tests
**Why:** `src/ui/commands.ts` has the most logic per LOC and zero coverage.
**Acceptance:** Tests for `handleModelCmd`, `handlePluginsCmd`, `handleMcpCmd`, `handleMemoryCmd` against mock managers.
**Labels:** testing
**Difficulty:** 🟡

---

## Bugs / cleanups

### BG1. Better error when `nomic-embed-text` not pulled
**Why:** Right now the assistant silently degrades to keyword search; the user can't tell.
**Acceptance:** First memory save without an embedding logs a one-time clear hint: "Semantic search off; pull nomic-embed-text in Ollama to enable."
**Labels:** bug, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### BG2. better-sqlite3 prebuild for Windows ARM64
**Why:** CI excludes Windows Node 20 already. ARM64 has no prebuild yet.
**Acceptance:** Document workaround in docs/INSTALL.md until upstream prebuild lands.
**Labels:** bug, documentation
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### BG3. Streaming render hiccup on very fast tokens
**Why:** Above ~100 tokens/sec, `renderMarkdown` on every chunk drops frames in Safari.
**Acceptance:** `requestAnimationFrame` coalesces renders; benchmark before/after.
**Labels:** bug, frontend, performance
**Difficulty:** 🟡

### BG4. Plugin loader: clearer error when entry export shape is wrong
**Why:** "module must export a PersonalAIPlugin as default or `plugin`" — fine but doesn't show what was exported.
**Acceptance:** Error includes `Object.keys(mod).join(', ')` and the keys that looked plausible.
**Labels:** bug, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### BG5. /clear should also reset the streaming state if invoked mid-response
**Why:** Edge case but it leaves the prompt confused.
**Acceptance:** `/clear` during streaming cancels the in-flight generator and clears.
**Labels:** bug
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### BG6. Memory `getStats` is O(N) over all rows
**Why:** Fine until 100 K memories; then it lags `/memory`.
**Acceptance:** Cache stats; invalidate on save/archive.
**Labels:** performance, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

### BG7. Session file disk usage report
**Why:** Sessions never auto-prune; the directory can grow.
**Acceptance:** `/sessions` shows total disk usage; CLI `/sessions prune` archives oldest beyond N.
**Labels:** enhancement, good first issue
**Difficulty:** 🟢

---

## Triage notes

- "good first issue" total above: ≥ 25. Aim to keep ≥ 10 open at all times.
- Hard items (🔴) should be designed in an issue thread before a PR opens —
  comment requesting design discussion first.
- Cross-area items (e.g., F11 = frontend + ai) belong to whichever area
  blocks the work; the second label is for filtering.
